title: unable to 'podman build' container image; selinux denials observed in journal
body:
/kind bug

On Fedora 28 Silverblue (`28.20180826.0`), I'm unable to use `podman build` to build a container.

```
$ rpm -q container-selinux podman
container-selinux-2.69-3.git452b90d.fc28.noarch
podman-0.8.3-1.git9d09a4d.fc28.x86_64

$ sudo podman build .
STEP 1: FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:28
STEP 2: WORKDIR /root/src
STEP 3: COPY . /root/src
STEP 4: RUN ./build.sh
standard_init_linux.go:191: exec user process caused "permission denied"
error building at step {Env:[DISTTAG=f28container FGC=f28  PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin] Command:run Args:[./build.sh] Flags:[] Attrs:map[] Message:RUN ./build.sh Original:RUN ./build.sh}: exit status 1

$ ls -latr
total 40
-rw-rw-r--.  1 miabbott miabbott   49 Jun 18 14:44 .gitignore
drwxrwxr-x. 12 miabbott miabbott  194 Aug 28 10:48 ..
-rw-rw-r--.  1 miabbott miabbott  414 Aug 28 10:49 README.md
-rw-rw-r--.  1 miabbott miabbott  208 Aug 28 10:49 Makefile
-rwxrwxr-x.  1 miabbott miabbott 5363 Aug 28 10:49 coreos-virt-install
-rwxrwxr-x.  1 miabbott miabbott 2253 Aug 28 10:49 coreos-oemid
-rwxrwxr-x.  1 miabbott miabbott  175 Aug 28 10:49 coreos-assembler.sh
-rw-rw-r--.  1 miabbott miabbott  200 Aug 28 10:49 .gitmodules
drwxrwxr-x.  2 miabbott miabbott    6 Aug 28 10:49 ostree-releng-scripts
drwxrwxr-x.  2 miabbott miabbott    6 Aug 28 10:49 mantle
-rw-rw-r--.  1 miabbott miabbott   91 Aug 28 10:49 Dockerfile
-rwxrwxr-x.  1 miabbott miabbott 2512 Aug 28 10:49 build.sh
drwxrwxr-x.  5 miabbott miabbott  239 Aug 28 10:49 .
drwxrwxr-x.  8 miabbott miabbott  220 Aug 28 10:53 .git


Aug 28 10:52:54 mastershake sudo[25063]: PAM unable to dlopen(/usr/lib64/security/pam_fprintd.so): /usr/lib64/security/pam_fprintd.so: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory
Aug 28 10:52:54 mastershake sudo[25063]: PAM adding faulty module: /usr/lib64/security/pam_fprintd.so
Aug 28 10:52:54 mastershake sudo[25063]: miabbott : TTY=pts/1 ; PWD=/var/home/miabbott/workspaces/coreos/coreos-assembler ; USER=root ; COMMAND=/bin/podman build .
Aug 28 10:52:54 mastershake audit[25063]: USER_ACCT pid=25063 uid=1000 auid=1000 ses=3 subj=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:unconfined_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 msg='op=PAM:accounting grantors=pam_unix,pam_localuser acct="miabbott" exe="/usr/bin/sudo" hostname=? addr=? terminal=/dev/pts/1 res=success'
Aug 28 10:52:54 mastershake audit[25063]: USER_CMD pid=25063 uid=1000 auid=1000 ses=3 subj=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:unconfined_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 msg='cwd="/var/home/miabbott/workspaces/coreos/coreos-assembler" cmd=706F646D616E206275696C64202E terminal=pts/1 res=success'
Aug 28 10:52:54 mastershake audit[25063]: CRED_REFR pid=25063 uid=0 auid=1000 ses=3 subj=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:unconfined_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 msg='op=PAM:setcred grantors=pam_env,pam_localuser,pam_unix acct="root" exe="/usr/bin/sudo" hostname=? addr=? terminal=/dev/pts/1 res=success'
Aug 28 10:52:54 mastershake sudo[25063]: pam_systemd(sudo:session): Cannot create session: Already running in a session
Aug 28 10:52:54 mastershake audit[25063]: USER_START pid=25063 uid=0 auid=1000 ses=3 subj=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:unconfined_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 msg='op=PAM:session_open grantors=pam_keyinit,pam_limits,pam_keyinit,pam_limits,pam_systemd,pam_unix acct="root" exe="/usr/bin/sudo" hostname=? addr=? terminal=/dev/pts/1 res=success'
Aug 28 10:52:54 mastershake sudo[25063]: pam_unix(sudo:session): session opened for user root by (uid=0)
Aug 28 10:52:55 mastershake kernel: SELinux: mount invalid.  Same superblock, different security settings for (dev mqueue, type mqueue)
Aug 28 10:52:55 mastershake audit[25169]: AVC avc:  denied  { entrypoint } for  pid=25169 comm="runc:[2:INIT]" path="/usr/bin/bash" dev="dm-2" ino=507883984 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c121,c576 tcontext=system_u:object_r:unlabeled_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=0
Aug 28 10:52:55 mastershake sudo[25063]: pam_unix(sudo:session): session closed for user root
Aug 28 10:52:55 mastershake audit[25063]: USER_END pid=25063 uid=0 auid=1000 ses=3 subj=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:unconfined_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 msg='op=PAM:session_close grantors=pam_keyinit,pam_limits,pam_keyinit,pam_limits,pam_systemd,pam_unix acct="root" exe="/usr/bin/sudo" hostname=? addr=? terminal=/dev/pts/1 res=success'
Aug 28 10:52:55 mastershake audit[25063]: CRED_DISP pid=25063 uid=0 auid=1000 ses=3 subj=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:unconfined_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 msg='op=PAM:setcred grantors=pam_env,pam_localuser,pam_unix acct="root" exe="/usr/bin/sudo" hostname=? addr=? terminal=/dev/pts/1 res=success'
```

An initial attempt to `restorecon -R -v /var/lib/containers` returned a lot of `could not set context...invalid argument` errors.

@rhatdan had me use `semodule -i /usr/share/selinux/packages/container.pp.bz2` and then re-run `restorecon`, which seemed to work this time around.

I observed a couple `not reset as customized by admin to system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c50,c585` messages and mostly `Relabled....from unconfined_u:object_r:unlabeled_t:s0 to unconfined_u:object_r:container_share_t:s0`

comments.body: This looks more like a SELinux labeling issue than `podman`, but I figure the right folks will see it.
Oh and after doing the `restorecon` dance, the `podman build` command worked.

This is an issue with silverblue, not being labeled correctly when it is built.  I think it is being bitten by the SELinux issue with libsemanage and container-selinux, which causes container-selinux to fail to install.

@cgwalters @dustymabe Can we get Silverblue updated?
I'll defer to @cgwalters - not sure what needs to be done exactly.
is this still a problem; else please close
I think I had managed to really mess up the labeling on my host.  I've got things back to a reasonable state and haven't encountered this again.
